note: Daily scrape for Wednesday 2026-05-06. Two new pr-marketing leads cleared the bar from the 5/5-5/6 window: (1) Blitzy — $200M growth round at $1.4B valuation 5/5 led by Northzone, Cambridge MA-based autonomous software development platform driving 5x engineering velocity for the world's largest enterprises (combined 84 — A-tier, fresh post-funding press cycle day 1); (2) CopilotKit — $27M Series A close 5/5 led by Glilot/NFX/SignalFire, Seattle-based AI agent infrastructure company whose AG-UI open standard has been adopted by Google/Microsoft/Amazon/Oracle with paying enterprise customers including Cisco, S&P Global, Docusign, Deutsche Telekom (combined 80 — A-tier border, day 1 of press cycle). Skipped: Sierra ($950M Series E 5/4 — late-stage Bret Taylor unicorn, $15B valuation, half of Fortune 50, established comms function — already skipped 5/5); Panthalassa ($140M Series B Thiel-led 5/5 — climate/fusion energy, out of FS/Tech scope); Cytospire ($83M Series A 5/5 — UK biotech, out of scope and non-US HQ); Standard Intelligence ($75M Sequoia/Spark 4/30 — frontier AI research lab, 6-person team founders age 20/21, no clear B2B sales motion at this stage, poor pr-marketing fit on buyer/sales-motion criterion); Moritz ($9M YC + 20VC 5/5 — AI-native law firm, oversubscribed in 4 days, but $9M YC-batch stage means budget for $5-25k/mo retainer is borderline-too-early and they ARE a law firm so legal-consulting buyer fit is inverted — they're the vendor not buyer); Hightouch ($150M Series D 4/27 announced — Goldman Sachs Alternatives + Bain Capital Ventures-led at $2.75B valuation, 9 days old, established Domino's/Autotrader/Cars.com/Aritzia/PetSmart customer roster suggests existing in-house comms or retained agency, window closed); Blitzy already covered above; True Anomaly ($600M defense — out of scope, already skipped 5/5); Reserv already in 5/5 report. Fund-formation, legal-consulting, coaching-ops, media, and ai-consulting pipelines surfaced no specific named-and-dated posts within the 72-hour rubric window — searches returned aggregator boards (Wellfound, Built In, ZipRecruiter, with-intelligence, walkersglobal). Same for legal-freelance and pr-freelance — Indeed/CareerBuilder/ZipRecruiter/LinkedIn returned generic aggregator listings without specific named-company role-and-date posts traceable to a 5/4-5/6 window. Signal refresh: Reserv (5/4 funding) Day 2 of press cycle, peak window 5/4-5/14, Mark should outreach NOW; Versana (4/30 funding) Day 6, window closing — last call for outreach in next 24-48 hours; Fun (5/1 raise) Day 5, agency-pitch window still hot through 5/11; JuliaHub Dyad 3.0 launch confirmed 5/19, agency-pitch sweet spot is RIGHT NOW (5/6, 13 days to launch); Rogo Day 7, post-peak press cycle, customer roster fully disclosed (35K users / 250 institutions — Rothschild/Jefferies/Lazard/Moelis/Nomura) and Felix agent product is published, late-stage outreach should pivot from 'breaking news' angle to 'category leadership' thought-leadership.

leads.name: Blitzy
leads.industry: Autonomous Software Development Platform for Enterprise Engineering / Agentic AI for Software Engineering Velocity (B2B Enterprise AI Tooling)
leads.fundingStage: $200M growth round announced May 5, 2026 at $1.4 billion valuation, led by Northzone, with new investors PSG, Battery Ventures, Jump Capital, Morgan Creek Digital, and Defiant, plus continued participation from existing backers Flybridge, Link Ventures, NFX, Picus Capital, Venture Guides, and strategic investments from Liberty Mutual Strategic Ventures, Erie Strategic Ventures, and BAL Ventures. Founded 2023 by CEO Brian Elliott (serial entrepreneur, former Army Ranger) and CTO Sid Pardeshi (former Nvidia master inventor, 27+ patents in neural networks and image generation).

contacts.name: Brian Elliott
contacts.title: Co-founder & CEO (press-active in 5/5 BusinessWire/SiliconANGLE/Crunchbase News coverage; serial entrepreneur, former Army Ranger)

contacts.name: Sid Pardeshi
contacts.title: Co-founder & CTO (former Nvidia master inventor, 27+ patents)

leads.source: BusinessWire (5/5) + SiliconANGLE (5/5) + Crunchbase News (5/5) + Finsmes (5/5) + Morningstar (5/5) + The SaaS News (5/5) + citybiz (5/5) + Pulse 2.0 (5/5) + NFX founder note (5/5) + Grit Daily (5/5)
leads.notes: Strongest new lead of the week. $200M at $1.4B valuation is a textbook growth-stage pr-marketing setup — they're now a unicorn with a category-defining narrative ('thousands of agents in parallel for days to weeks of uninterrupted inference' / '5x engineering velocity for some of the world's largest enterprises'). The combination of (a) Northzone as the named lead — Northzone is a high-credibility European/US growth fund that typically expects portfolio companies to invest in tier-1 PR — and (b) the strategic-LP corner of the cap table (Liberty Mutual Strategic Ventures, Erie Strategic Ventures, BAL Ventures) means there's both implicit press expectation AND existing customer/insurer credibility built into the funding announcement. Buyer fit is strong: Blitzy sells B2B to enterprise engineering organizations (Fortune-500-sized buyers) with a quantifiable proof point ('5x engineering velocity') and a clear category claim ('autonomous software development for the enterprise'). Budget at unicorn stage with $200M in dry powder is the highest-credibility tier — agency retainers in this segment run $25-$50k/mo and often $50k+ for category-creator narratives. Comms-relationship odds: STRONG. The 5/5 BusinessWire release lists no Head of Comms or VP Marketing quote — only CEO Brian Elliott and the Northzone partner — which is the same signal we saw on Reserv 5/4 (likely junior in-house comms, fractional, or vendor-managed). Recommend a 90-day pitch threading: (a) the 'autonomous software development is the next platform shift' category-creation narrative — Cursor/Cognition/Devin are competitors but Blitzy's 'thousands of agents in parallel for weeks' framing is differentiated, (b) the founder-origin story (Pardeshi as ex-Nvidia master inventor with 27+ patents is press-gold for the engineering trade), (c) the unicorn valuation as 'enterprise AI dev tooling has graduated from hype to validated category' angle for Forbes/WSJ/FT enterprise software beats, (d) the customer-velocity thought-leadership track ('case study' format with anonymized 5x-improvement claim — this needs at least one named case study to land in IT/CIO trade press). Time-sensitive: Series-D-equivalent press cycles run 7-14 days max, clock started 5/5, so peak window is 5/5-5/15 (we're on day 2). Outreach in the next 48 hours is optimal. Lead-touch at CEO Elliott since he's the named press contact in the 5/5 release. Skip ai-consulting (Blitzy is the vendor not the consulting buyer); skip legal-consulting/legal-freelance/pr-freelance (no relevant signal). Combined score 84 — A-tier, strongest new pr-marketing lead since Reserv (5/4, 87) and Fun (5/1, 88). Cambridge MA is in-network (Mark's been on Boston/Cambridge campus circuits before). Category-defining trigger event.

leads.name: CopilotKit
leads.industry: AI Agent Infrastructure / Open-Standard Interface Layer for Embedding AI Agents into SaaS Applications (B2B Developer Infrastructure / Enterprise AI Tooling)
leads.fundingStage: $27M combined financing announced May 5, 2026 — new $20M Series A led by Glilot Capital, NFX, and SignalFire, plus a previously closed $7M seed. The Series A funds the build-out of AG-UI, the open standard CopilotKit created for how AI agents communicate with software applications.

contacts.name: Atai Barkai
contacts.title: Co-founder & CEO (CopilotKit; press-active in TechCrunch and GeekWire 5/5 coverage)

leads.source: TechCrunch (5/5) + GeekWire (5/5) + Tech Startups May 5 (5/5) + Calcalist (5/5) + Ynet (5/5) + Rolling Out (5/5) + AI Funding Tracker (5/5)
leads.notes: Strong A-tier border candidate, day 1 of press cycle. Two narrative threads make CopilotKit unusually compelling for a Series A pr-marketing pitch: (a) the AG-UI open-standard angle is a textbook category-creator framing — they invented and own the 'how do AI agents talk to software applications' protocol, and the four hyperscalers (Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Oracle) have ALL adopted it, which is press-gold for the 'invisible infrastructure' beat that Forbes/Bloomberg/WSJ enterprise tech writers love; (b) the customer roster — Cisco, S&P Global, Docusign, Deutsche Telekom — is unusually heavy for a Series A and screams 'we have actual revenue, not just hype.' Buyer fit is strong on the proof and B2B-buyer dimensions (10/9) but weaker on budget (7/10) — a $27M Series A means they're funded for product/eng spend but agency retainers in the $5-15k/mo tier are realistic, $25-50k retainers would be a stretch. Comms-relationship odds: MODERATE-STRONG. The TechCrunch/GeekWire 5/5 coverage is CEO-led (Atai Barkai is the named press contact) with no Head of Comms or VP Marketing quote — typical Series A signal. Recommend a 90-day pitch threading: (a) the 'open-standard for AI agents' category-creation narrative, leaning hard on the four-hyperscaler adoption as the lede (Google + Microsoft + Amazon + Oracle is a unique press hook), (b) the developer-to-enterprise motion ('we started as a dev tool, now Cisco/S&P/Docusign/Deutsche Telekom run on it') as a Built In SF / TechCrunch Strictly VC angle, (c) the AG-UI standard-body framing — pitch it as 'the HTML for AI agents' for trade press that wants a clean analogy, (d) the founder-origin story if Barkai's background fits a press-friendly narrative (likely Israeli-founder cross-Atlantic angle for TechCrunch/CTech). Caveats: (i) Glilot/NFX/SignalFire are well-known and well-resourced VCs that may have captive PR support — Mark would need to differentiate against the standard YC/early-stage VC PR playbook, (ii) Series A budget tier means scoping a smaller initial engagement ($10-15k/mo) is more realistic than the $25-50k tier we see at Series C+, (iii) competing platforms (Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, etc.) are also fundraising in this category — narrative differentiation matters more than usual. Time-sensitive: Series A press cycles run 5-10 days max, clock started 5/5, peak window is 5/5-5/13. Outreach in next 48-72 hours is optimal. Lead-touch at CEO Barkai. Skip ai-consulting (CopilotKit is the vendor); skip legal-* / coaching-* / media (no relevant signal). Combined score 80 — A-tier border. Seattle HQ is out-of-network for Mark's NY base but the company has Israeli founding team that often relocates to NY for biz dev — worth a 20-min call to assess.
